run_name: NATP Module 3: Communication/Interpersonal Skills — Source-First Dry Run
purpose: The purpose of this unit is to introduce concepts and skills required for the Nurse Assistant to communicate effectively and interact appropriately with patients/residents, patient's/residents' families and guests, and other members of the health care team. Terminology 19. Non-verbal communication 1. Anger 20. Personal space 2. Aphasia 21. Physiological 3. Basic human need 22. Projection 4. Body language 23. Psychosocial 5. Communication 24. Rationalization 6. Conflict 25. Receiver 7. Conversion 26. Regression 8. Defense mechanisms 27. Report 9. Denial 28. Repression 10. Displacement 29. Sender 11. Dyslexia 30. Sublimation 12. Dysphasia 31. Substitution 13. Empathy 32. Sympathy 14. Family 33. Verbal communication 15. Identification 34. Voice pitch 16. Medical chart 35. Voice tone 17. Message 18. Myth

objectives.objective_title: Describe effective communication/interpersonal skills used with patients/residents, their families and guests.
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Describe effective communication/interpersonal skills used with patients/residents, their families and guests.
objectives.source_excerpt: : A. Lecture/Discussion A. Written test Describe effective Communication/Interpersonal Skills used with patients/residents, their families and guests. A. Communication/Interpersonal Skills for use with a patient/resident, family, or guest 1. Introduce self 2. Use the patient's/resident's formal name initially 3. After initial introductions, ask patient/resident how he or she would like to be addressed. Add this information to the nursing care plan so all staff members have the information 4. Explain all tasks to the patient/resident before doing them 5. Be patient and a good listener 6. Use short sentences, ask for feedback 7. Use eye contact 8. Speak clearly, avoid criticizing, and avoid interrupting 9. Clarify information or conversation as needed 10. Be aware of body language of self and others 11. Verbal and non-verbal messages should agree 12. Use words that are easily understood 13. Show interest and respect 14. Use a friendly tone of voice 15. Be positive while being appropriate to each interaction 16. Ensure confidentiality Module 3: Communication/Interpersonal Skills B. Additional skills when communicating with patients/residents B. Role play activity: B. Demonstrates use of with special needs Communication effective communication 1. Language/cultural differences Challenges strategies and actions a. Ask for an interpreter if the patient/resident or family 1. Divide into groups of with patient/resident, speaks little or no English or the language of the Nurse 4-5 students families, guests, and Assistant Assign each other healthcare b. Know cultural beliefs and pract

objectives.source_excerpt: Describe communication between the members of the health care team. A. Health Care Communication 1. Methods of communicating a. Verbal b. Non-verbal c. Written: chart, patient summary/care plan, report sheets, Activity of Daily Living form, and weight and vital signs forms d. Electronic: computer, fax, telephone, intercom system 2. Legal aspects of communications a. Must document what has been reported verbally to licensed nurse b. Must document statements heard from the patient/resident and family that are important Module 3: Communication/Interpersonal Skills B. Rules for effective communication 1. Identify self by name and title in any form of communication 2. Verbal reports should be a. Brief b. Organized c. Appropriate and focused 1) Diagnosis 2) Allergies 3) Activity and tolerance 4) Elimination 5) Special needs 6) Diet and appetite 7) Vital signs and weight 8) Code status d. Timely e. Respectful of patient/resident confidentiality 3. Telephone communication a. Take notes during the call b. Name of person the message is for c. Verify correct spelling of caller's name d. Indicate time of call e. Clarify the message with the caller by repeating it and repeating the telephone number f. Clearly sign full name and title to the message 4. Answering a patient/resident call signal a. Answer promptly, quietly, and in a friendly manner b. If an intercom is used, call the patient/resident by name, identify self and politely inquire as to the patient's/resident's need c. Make sure the patient/resident can always reach the call light Module 3: Communication/Interpersonal Skills
